text:
SURGICAL PATHOLOGY REPORT

PATIENT: Okafor, Denise
ACCESSION NUMBER: SP-XXXX-XXXXX
ATTENDING PATHOLOGIST: [Pathologist on service]
CLINICAL SERVICE: Breast Surgery / Oncology

---

SPECIMEN SUBMITTED:
A: Left breast, upper outer quadrant — ultrasound-guided core needle biopsy (14-gauge, 3 cores)
B: Left axilla — ultrasound-guided core needle biopsy, sentinel lymph node sampling (needle biopsy of level I node, 2 passes)

DATE OF PROCEDURE: [Date recorded in procedure log]
DATE OF FINAL REPORT: [To be populated programmatically]
CLINICAL INDICATION: 49-year-old woman with palpable left breast mass, upper outer quadrant. Mammogram and MRI demonstrate 3.2 x 2.8 cm irregular spiculated mass with type III enhancement curve, BIRADS 5. Left axillary adenopathy identified on imaging. Biopsy for tissue diagnosis.

---

FINAL DIAGNOSIS:

A. LEFT BREAST, UPPER OUTER QUADRANT, ULTRASOUND-GUIDED CORE NEEDLE BIOPSY:
   — INVASIVE DUCTAL CARCINOMA (INFILTRATING DUCT CARCINOMA, NOS), POORLY DIFFERENTIATED
   — Nottingham Histologic Grade: 3/3
       Tubule formation: 3/3 (minimal/absent)
       Nuclear pleomorphism: 3/3 (marked)
       Mitotic count: 3/3 (>20 mitoses per 10 high-power fields at 40x)
       Total Nottingham score: 9/9
   — Associated high-grade ductal carcinoma in situ (DCIS), solid and comedo patterns,
     with central necrosis (approximately 10% of biopsy specimen)
   — Lymphovascular invasion: NOT IDENTIFIED on biopsy cores
   — Stromal tumor-infiltrating lymphocytes (TILs): approximately 35% (per IBEX/TIL WG criteria)
   — See ancillary studies for immunohistochemistry results

B. LEFT AXILLA, ULTRASOUND-GUIDED CORE NEEDLE BIOPSY (LEVEL I LYMPH NODE):
   — METASTATIC CARCINOMA, CONSISTENT WITH BREAST PRIMARY
   — Largest deposit: approximately 4 mm
   — Extranodal extension: NOT IDENTIFIED on biopsy material

---

GROSS DESCRIPTION:

Specimen A: Received in formalin, labeled "left breast UOQ core biopsy." Three cylindrical core biopsy fragments are present, each measuring approximately 1.4 to 1.6 cm in length and 0.15 cm in diameter. The cores are tan-white, firm, with a gritty cut surface on sectioning. All three cores are submitted entirely in one cassette, processed with standard FFPE protocol. Representative H&E sections prepared at 3 levels.

Specimen B: Received in formalin, labeled "left axilla FNA/core — level I node." Two core biopsy fragments present, measuring 0.9 and 1.1 cm in length. The tissue is tan-pink and soft. Entirely submitted in one cassette.

---

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:

Specimen A — Left Breast Core Biopsy:
Sections show breast parenchyma extensively replaced by a poorly differentiated invasive carcinoma arranged in solid nests, trabeculae, and individually dispersed cells within a desmoplastic stroma. The tumor cells are large with markedly irregular nuclei, coarse chromatin, and conspicuous macronucleoli. The cytoplasm is moderate and eosinophilic. Mitotic figures are numerous and easily identified, with atypical forms present; mitotic count exceeds 20 per 10 HPF (40x objective). Areas of central geographic necrosis are noted within both the invasive component and the associated in situ component. The background stroma demonstrates a moderate lymphoplasmacytic infiltrate; stromal TILs are estimated at approximately 35% by standardized scoring.

At the periphery of the biopsy cores, foci of high-grade DCIS are identified with solid and cribriform architecture and central comedo-type necrosis. The DCIS occupies approximately 10% of the biopsy material. The ductal basement membrane appears largely intact on routine sections.

No unequivocal lymphovascular invasion (angiolymphatic emboli) is identified within the submitted biopsy cores. No perineural invasion is seen.

Specimen B — Left Axillary Core Biopsy:
Sections show residual lymph node tissue with largely effaced architecture. There are clusters and sheets of malignant epithelial cells with cytomorphology identical to the invasive carcinoma in Specimen A. The largest contiguous cluster measures approximately 4 mm. No definitive extranodal extension is identified on the submitted sections. The background lymph node tissue shows reactive sinus histiocytosis.

---

ANCILLARY STUDIES — IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY (performed on Specimen A, FFPE block A1):

All IHC performed on the Leica BOND-III autostainer with appropriate positive and negative controls running concurrently. ASCO/CAP 2020 guidelines applied for ER/PR and HER2 interpretation.

  Estrogen Receptor (ER), clone SP1 (Ventana):
    Result: NEGATIVE
    Staining: 0% of invasive tumor nuclei stain positive
    Allred Score: 0/8 (proportion score 0, intensity score 0)
    Internal positive control (adjacent normal ducts): positive — PASS

  Progesterone Receptor (PR), clone 1E2 (Ventana):
    Result: NEGATIVE
    Staining: 0% of invasive tumor nuclei stain positive
    Allred Score: 0/8
    Internal positive control: positive — PASS

  HER2 (c-erbB-2), clone 4B5 (Ventana):
    Result: NEGATIVE (Score 1+)
    Pattern: Incomplete, faint, basolateral membranous staining in <10% of tumor cells
    Interpretation: Score 1+ per ASCO/CAP 2018 guidelines; does NOT meet criteria for HER2-low (1+ without reflex FISH)
    Note: FISH reflexed per institutional protocol for cases 2+ only; FISH not indicated for IHC 1+
    (Addendum note: FISH performed per clinician request — HER2/CEP17 ratio 1.4; average HER2 copy number 2.1 signals/cell; NOT AMPLIFIED)

  Ki-67 (MIB-1, Dako):
    Result: 85% nuclear positivity (hot spot method; global average approximately 82%)
    Interpretation: High proliferative index

  CK5/6 (clone D5/16B4):
    Result: Positive (diffuse, moderate-to-strong cytoplasmic staining)
    Interpretation: Consistent with basal-like phenotype

  EGFR (clone 31G7):
    Result: Positive (2+, membranous, approximately 60% of cells)
    Interpretation: Overexpression; basal-like pattern

  p53 (clone DO-7):
    Result: Diffuse strong nuclear positivity (>80% of cells)
    Interpretation: Aberrant/mutant-type p53 expression pattern

  E-cadherin (clone 36/E-cadherin):
    Result: Positive (intact membranous staining)
    Interpretation: Consistent with ductal/no evidence of lobular differentiation

PD-L1 IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY:
  PD-L1, clone 22C3 (Dako PharmDx assay, performed as CLIA-validated LDT):
    Combined Positive Score (CPS): 18
    Method: CPS = (number of PD-L1 staining cells [tumor cells + lymphocytes + macrophages] / total viable tumor cells) x 100
    Interpretation: CPS 18 — POSITIVE by KEYNOTE-522 eligibility criteria (CPS ≥10)
    Quality control: adequate, >100 viable tumor cells evaluated

---

SUMMARY / BIOMARKER PROFILE:

  Receptor status:    ER negative / PR negative / HER2 negative (IHC 1+; FISH ratio 1.4, not amplified)
  Molecular subtype:  Triple-negative; basal-like phenotype (CK5/6+, EGFR+)
  Proliferative index: Ki-67 85% (markedly elevated)
  PD-L1 CPS:         18 (positive)
  Stromal TILs:       35%
  p53:                Mutant-pattern overexpression

---

PATHOLOGIST'S NOTE:
The morphologic and immunophenotypic findings are diagnostic of poorly differentiated (grade 3) invasive ductal carcinoma (infiltrating duct carcinoma, NOS) of the left breast, triple-negative subtype. The high Ki-67 proliferative index, markedly elevated mitotic rate, associated high-grade DCIS with comedo necrosis, and CK5/6/EGFR positivity are all consistent with a basal-like molecular subtype. The PD-L1 CPS of 18 by 22C3 assay and the moderate stromal TIL density of 35% are noted for clinical correlation. Left axillary lymph node core biopsy confirms regional nodal metastasis. Results have been communicated to the managing clinician. Germline testing has been recommended given patient age, triple-negative subtype, and family history.

Electronic sign-out by attending pathologist of record.

---

Tissue block availability: FFPE blocks A1 and B1 available in the Department of Pathology tissue archive for additional studies as clinically indicated.
